retrieved_docs.title: Pulmonary Veno-Occlusive Disease -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: Autoimmune and connective disorders such as systemic sclerosis (SSc) are well-known causes of PAH and pulmonary hypertension from interstitial lung disease (PH-ILD). [24] Recently, PVOD has been hypothesized to be the reason for the treatment-refractory nature of these patients. A study from 2007 compared lung samples from 8 cases (5 autopsy samples and three explant samples after lung transplant) with connective tissue diseases to 29 patients with no history of connective tissue diseases. The study found that 6 out of 8 patients (75%) with connective tissue diseases had histology consistent with PVOD as opposed to 5 out of 29 patients (17.2%) with no connective tissue disease. [24]

retrieved_docs.title: Case Study: 33-Year-Old Female Presents with Chronic SOB and Cough (Archived) -- Case Presentation
retrieved_docs.content: Social History: Her tobacco use is 33 pack-years; however, she quit smoking shortly prior to the onset of symptoms, six months ago. She denies alcohol and illicit drug use. She is in a married, monogamous relationship and has three children aged 15 months to 5 years. She is employed in a cookie bakery. She has two pet doves. She traveled to Mexico for a one-week vacation one year ago. Allergies: No known medicine, food, or environmental allergies. Past Medical History: Hypertension Past Surgical History: Cholecystectomy Medications: Lisinopril 10 mg by mouth every day

retrieved_docs.title: Case Study: 33-Year-Old Female Presents with Chronic SOB and Cough (Archived) -- Discussion -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: If not treated appropriately and in a timely fashion, the disease can be fatal, and complications will arise, such as recurrent pneumonia leading to respiratory failure, superior vena cava syndrome, fibrosing mediastinitis, pulmonary vessel obstruction leading to pulmonary hypertension and right-sided heart failure, and progressive fibrosis of lymph nodes. Acute pulmonary histoplasmosis usually has a good outcome on symptomatic therapy alone, with 90% of patients being asymptomatic. Disseminated histoplasmosis, if untreated, results in death within 2 to 24 months. Overall, there is a relapse rate of 50% in acute disseminated histoplasmosis. In chronic treatment, however, this relapse rate decreases to 10% to 20%. Death is imminent without treatment.

retrieved_docs.title: Chronic Emphysema(Archived) -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: The differential diagnosis for COPD is very broad as the symptoms are nonspecific. The obstructive ventilatory defect is also present in bronchial asthma. Fixed obstruction also presents in long-standing asthma without coexisting COPD. Many chronic respiratory diseases like pulmonary fibrosis, bronchiectasis, cardiac failure, pulmonary hypertension, pulmonary vascular diseases, share many common symptoms. Infectious diseases like pneumonia, tuberculosis, fungal infections; inflammatory disorders like interstitial pneumonia can all produce chronic cough. In elderly patients and those with neurological diseases, chronic aspiration should be a therapeutic option. Nonpulmonary diseases like severe acid reflux disease, upper airways diseases like chronic rhinosinusitis can be in the differential diagnosis in appropriate clinical situations.

retrieved_docs.title: Farmer's Lung(Archived) -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: A high index of suspicion, along with exposure history, high-resolution computerized tomography (HRCT) scan, and marked lymphocytosis are the main factors leading to the diagnosis of farmer's lung. [11] HRCT is a useful investigation in evaluating acute and chronic cases. Acute features are diffuse ground-glass opacities, centrilobular nodules, mosaic pattern, and air trapping. Chronic features include septal thickening, ground-glass opacities, fibrosis, bronchiectasis, usually with upper lobe predominance, and signs of pulmonary hypertension. Some patients may present with emphysema, and this may be a confounding feature in smokers. [12]

retrieved_docs.content: CT of the chest was performed to further the pulmonary diagnosis; it showed a diffuse centrilobular micronodular pattern without focal consolidation.

retrieved_docs.title: Chronic Emphysema(Archived) -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: A typical patient with emphysema would be in their fifth decade or over, with extensive smoking history presenting with a chronic, productive cough and worsening shortness of breath. In advanced COPD, the symptoms progressively worsen interfering with the activities of daily living. They may also experience unintentional weight loss and would appear cachexic. In acute exacerbations, patients decompensate and usually have a cough with a copious amount of phlegm, extensive wheezing, and progress to hypercarbia with associated hypoxic respiratory failure. Patients typically demonstrate 'purse lip breathing' and can be visibly tachypneic and use their accessory muscles of respiration. Auscultation usually reveals reduced air entry. Cor pulmonale is a complication of long-standing emphysema and could cause symptoms and signs suggestive of right heart failure. The term 'pink puffers' is used to describe these patients as they are plethoric and have hyperexpanded (barrel-shaped) chest. They are not cyanotic in milder stages. On the contrary, patients with decompensated chronic bronchitis are referred to as 'blue bloaters' as they are cyanotic. Hemoptysis is very rare in COPD, and if present, more serious conditions including malignancy should be a consideration in an appropriate clinical setting.

retrieved_docs.title: Adenocarcinoma(Archived) -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: Adenocarcinoma of the lung can present with a chronic dry cough, dyspnea, hemoptysis, weight loss, or as an incidental finding on routine imaging. Lung adenocarcinoma is associated with extrapulmonary features like hypertrophic osteoarthropathy, which manifests as clubbing, periostitis of long bones, and arthritis.

retrieved_docs.title: Secondary Pulmonary Hypertension -- Pearls and Other Issues
retrieved_docs.content: Chronic lung diseases are a common cause of pulmonary hypertension but it almost never increases the pulmonary artery pressure more than 50 mm Hg and in most cases only causes mild pulmonary hypertension. Therefore, evaluate for other causes if pressure is markedly increased and goes beyond 50 mm Hg.

retrieved_docs.content: الأشعة السينية على الصَدْر غير طبيعية are clinically associated with تَشَمَّعٌ كُحولِيّ and with تعاطي الكحول. The presence of an abnormal chest X‑ray may therefore suggest underlying alcohol‑related pulmonary pathology, such as alcoholic emphysema, and should prompt clinicians to evaluate the patient’s history of alcohol consumption. Recognizing these associations aids in accurate diagnosis and targeted management of patients with suspected alcohol‑related lung disease.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 33 -- Level 3 -- Community 1496
retrieved_docs.content: Primary hypertension (فرط ضغط الدم الأساسي) commonly presents with hemodynamic abnormalities such as elevated mean arterial pressure and elevated systolic blood pressure, both of which are recognized manifestations of the condition. In addition to these direct cardiovascular signs, primary hypertension is associated with primary pulmonary hypertension (ارتفاع ضغط الدم الرِئَوِيّ الأَوَّلِيّ), indicating a link between systemic and pulmonary vascular pressure elevations. Patients with primary hypertension may also exhibit abnormal findings on chest radiography (الأشعة السينية على الصَدْر غير طبيعية), a clinical association that can aid in the diagnostic evaluation and monitoring of the disease. Recognizing these interconnected manifestations and associations is essential for comprehensive assessment and management of individuals with primary hypertension.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 4 -- Community 2926
retrieved_docs.content: Elevated mean arterial pressure and elevated systolic blood pressure are both classified as vascular abnormalities, reflecting increased pressure within the circulatory system. The broader vascular category also encompasses severe pressure disorders such as أزمة فرط ضغط الدم (hypertensive crisis) and ارتفاع ضغط الدم الرِئَوِيّ الأَوَّلِيّ (primary pulmonary hypertension). Together, these entities illustrate a spectrum of vascular pressure elevations that are important for clinicians to recognize, as they can signal acute emergencies or chronic pulmonary vascular disease and guide appropriate monitoring and therapeutic strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 3 -- Community 1936
retrieved_docs.content: Elevated mean arterial pressure, hypertensive crisis (أزمة فرط ضغط الدم), primary pulmonary hypertension (ارتفاع ضغط الدم الرِئَوِيّ الأَوَّلِيّ), and reactive hypertension (فرط ضغط الدم الانتيابي) are all classified within the broader vascular category. These conditions represent distinct manifestations of abnormal vascular pressure regulation. Elevated mean arterial pressure reflects a sustained increase in the average arterial pressure throughout the cardiac cycle. Hypertensive crisis denotes an acute, severe elevation of blood pressure that can threaten organ function if not promptly treated. Primary pulmonary hypertension is a specific form of high pressure that originates in the pulmonary arteries, leading to right‑ventricular strain and potential respiratory complications. Reactive hypertension describes a secondary rise in systemic blood pressure that occurs in response to another underlying condition. Recognizing that these entities share a common vascular basis aids clinicians in assessing, differentiating, and managing patients with hypertension‑related disorders, thereby improving diagnostic accuracy and therapeutic outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 3 -- Community 1701
retrieved_docs.content: Respiratory failure; insufficiency; arrest (adult) is a specific adult respiratory condition that is classified under the broader category of اضطراب تَنَفُّسِيّ (respiratory disorder).

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 4 -- Community 2872
retrieved_docs.content: Elevated diastolic blood pressure, elevated mean arterial pressure, and elevated systolic blood pressure are all classified as vascular conditions, and the vascular category also encompasses primary pulmonary hypertension (ارتفاع ضغط الدم الرِئَوِيّ الأَوَّلِيّ). These entities represent distinct manifestations of abnormal pressure within the circulatory system, illustrating how various forms of hypertension and pulmonary vascular disease fall under the broader umbrella of vascular pathology. Recognizing and managing these vascular pressure abnormalities is essential for comprehensive patient care.

The class includes acetylcysteine inhalation solutions (100 mg/mL and 200 mg/mL) and the broader category “expectorants, excl. combinations with cough suppressants.” By reducing mucus viscosity, mucolytics facilitate airway clearance in respiratory conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 4 -- Community 2868
retrieved_docs.content: Abnormal chest X‑ray findings (الأشعة السينية على الصَدْر غير طبيعية) are clinically associated with alcohol consumption (تعاطي الكحول). In patients who consume alcohol, clinicians should consider that pulmonary imaging may reveal atypical or pathological changes, which can stem from alcohol‑related lung injury, aspiration events, infections, or other respiratory complications. Recognizing this association prompts healthcare providers to inquire about alcohol use when interpreting abnormal chest radiographs, to assess for potential alcohol‑related pulmonary disease, and to incorporate appropriate counseling or treatment for alcohol misuse into the overall patient care plan.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 4 -- Community 2753
retrieved_docs.content: Elevated systolic blood pressure is classified as a vascular condition, and it belongs to the same category as several other high‑pressure disorders. Within the vascular domain, أزمة فرط ضغط الدم (hypertensive crisis) represents an acute, severe elevation of blood pressure that requires immediate medical intervention. ارتفاع ضغط الدم الرِئَوِيّ الأَوَّلِيّ (primary pulmonary hypertension) denotes a chronic increase in pressure within the pulmonary arteries that occurs without an identifiable secondary cause. فرط ضغط الدم الانقباضي (systolic hypertension) describes a pattern in which only the systolic component of arterial pressure is elevated. Together, Elevated systolic blood pressure, hypertensive crisis, primary pulmonary hypertension, and systolic hypertension are all considered child entities of the broader Vascular classification, highlighting their shared relevance to vascular pathology and the importance of recognizing and managing these distinct forms of high blood pressure in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 3 -- Community 1531
retrieved_docs.content: Elevated mean arterial pressure is classified as a vascular condition that reflects an overall increase in arterial pressure. Within the broader vascular category, several specific hypertensive disorders are recognized as subtypes, including أزمة فرط ضغط الدم (hypertensive crisis), ارتفاع ضغط الدم الرِئَوِيّ الأَوَّلِيّ (primary pulmonary hypertension), and فرط ضغط الدم الانقباضي (systolic hypertension). Although these entities share the common feature of elevated blood pressure, they differ in their underlying mechanisms, affected vascular territories, and clinical manifestations. Recognizing that elevated mean arterial pressure and its vascular subtypes are interrelated helps clinicians accurately diagnose, monitor, and tailor treatment strategies for patients presenting with various forms of hypertension, thereby improving patient care and outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 4 -- Community 2894
retrieved_docs.content: Primary hypertension (فرط ضغط الدم الأساسي) commonly manifests as elevated diastolic blood pressure and elevated systolic blood pressure. In addition to these classic blood‑pressure findings, primary hypertension is associated with primary pulmonary hypertension (ارتفاع ضغط الدم الرِئَوِيّ الأَوَّلِيّ), indicating a link between systemic and pulmonary vascular pressure elevations. Clinically, primary hypertension is also linked to gout (مرض النقرس), reflecting an association between sustained high blood pressure and metabolic‑inflammatory disorders. Recognizing these interconnections aids clinicians in comprehensive assessment and management of patients with hypertension and its related systemic complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 3 -- Community 1507
retrieved_docs.content: Elevated diastolic blood pressure and elevated mean arterial pressure are vascular abnormalities that belong to the broader category of Vascular disorders. Within the Vascular category, additional child conditions include أزمة فرط ضغط الدم (hypertensive crisis) and ارتفاع ضغط الدم الرِئَوِيّ الأَوَّلِيّ (primary pulmonary hypertension). Recognizing that these various forms of elevated arterial pressure—whether systemic diastolic, mean arterial, hypertensive crisis, or primary pulmonary hypertension—are interconnected components of vascular pathology is important for accurate diagnosis and comprehensive patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 2 -- Community 1246
retrieved_docs.content: Essential hypertension (فرط ضغط الدم الأساسي or primary hypertension) is interpreted through the concept of blood pressure (ضغط الدم) and includes both systolic hypertension (فرط ضغط الدم الانقباضي) and diastolic hypertension (فرط ضغط الدم الانتيابي), as well as elevated mean arterial pressure; each is classified as a vascular finding. Essential hypertension is a central node that links vascular manifestations (including hypertensive crisis أزمة فرط ضغط الدم), comorbid conditions such as gout (مرض النقرس) and primary pulmonary hypertension (ارتفاع ضغط الدم الرِئَوِيّ الأَوَّلِيّ), and serious outcomes recorded in the CPTAC Causes of Death codelist (e.g., aortic, peripheral, and visceral artery aneurysms). It is closely related to renal vascular pathology, exemplified by benign arteriolar nephrosclerosis (اضطراب الأوعية الكلوية) and malignant arteriolar nephrosclerosis, both of which are clinically similar to hypertension with complications and secondary hypertension.
